entries.content: InTheWake is where the website work happens; ken is the tooling (orchestrator, skills, cognitive memory). When entering this household, the operator's standing onboarding instruction is: read .claude/, settings, voice rules, careful-not-clever especially, the admin/claude/ documentation, and the .claude/skills/ directory before doing anything else. "Explore the repository" is not optional throat-clearing — it's a guardrail against acting on stale assumptions.

verification_meta.rationale: Legacy extract — not yet re-governed under Academy admission.

entries.content: When building a tool plus its companion article in InTheWake, the operator's preferred sequence is: (1) brainstorming pass, (2) original research on every dollar figure / percent / fact from primary sources, (3) build the tool, (4) re-shape the article around the live tool. Do NOT build the tool strictly from the article — the article is a guideline, not a spec. If the article presupposes the tool's existence, preserve the original article draft to admin/ before rewriting, save the discarded prose for reference, and write the new article only after the tool ships.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: The current voice template for new InTheWake articles is articles/caribbean-cruise-trends-2026.html — pastoral, observational, calm-authority register. Mirror its page-shell pattern, byline placement, and footer pattern. The like-a-human skill enforces voice during drafting; voice-audit runs as the post-draft gate. Drafts are not shippable until voice-audit passes. The InTheWake voice rejects cruise-marketing tropes ("sun-soaked decks", "sail confidently", "your dream cruise") and tone-flat-out language ("ensure", "dive in", "master with confidence").

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: InTheWake never uses training-data as a factual source. Every dollar figure, percent, rate, exemption age, ship stat, or factual claim must trace to (a) the cruise line's official policy page, (b) a contemporary trade press source (Cruise Critic, Cruise Hive, Cruzely, Eat Sleep Cruise), or (c) the repository's own JSON data files. When a claim cannot be sourced, mark BLOCKED — do not fill it in from memory. Concrete disqualification: the "Forbes / 95% of crew take-home from gratuities" claim traces to Cruise Critic with hedged wording, not Forbes; cite Cruise Critic with the hedge, not Forbes flat.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: When multiple unfinished items remain, the operator's rule is: document ALL of them in admin/UNFINISHED_TASKS.md first, then proceed by user-trust impact — not by what's easiest, fastest, or most architecturally interesting. "User-trust" means: does the tool give correct dollar amounts? Does the page show correct facts? Other dimensions (discovery, visibility, refactor cleanliness, defensive process work) come after correctness for end users. The UNFINISHED_TASKS log is non-negotiable: items not documented there get silently lost when context summarizes.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: Before declaring a piece of work done, fetch origin/main and merge, then read the current CLAUDE.md and any upgraded skill it references (especially careful-not-clever), then audit the shipped work against the upgraded standards. The careful-not-clever skill's promotion of v1.7-alpha to canonical on 2026-05-09 was a concrete example: work that shipped under v1.0 had to be re-audited against the new Layer 2/Layer 3 requirements. The audit is the gate, not a nice-to-have — the operator will call it out by name if it's skipped.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: When the operator insists a file or resource exists ("it's up there", "check again", "fetch main") and Claude has been refusing, the default assumption is that the operator is correct and Claude has been checking the wrong location. STOP arguing. Specifically: fetch origin/main and inspect the remote tree before insisting a file isn't reachable. The 2026-05-09 session had Claude refuse 4+ times that an image.jpg was on disk; it was at the repo root on main exactly where the operator said. Sharp operator language is a strong redirect signal — read it as "you're overcomplicating something simple; fetch the obvious source of truth and try again."

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: When adding new articles or pages to InTheWake, the operator's standing QA questions are: "are all the articles showing up unique?" and "are they using the full site shell?" The expected answers come from a data-driven rail manifest (assets/data/articles/index.json), not manual per-page edits. If the answer is "I manually updated 1,300 rails," that's a bug — the rail should be data-driven so adding an entry once propagates everywhere. Surface this concern proactively when shipping article work; do not wait for the operator to ask.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.

entries.content: The cruise-tipping calculator's schema (assets/data/tipping/_schema.json v1.1) is locked into an additive-extension strategy: optional fields cover per-line quirks without forcing migration of single-tier, single-region lines. Concrete extensions in place: dailyRates.tiers[] for multi-tier cabin pricing (Princess 3-tier, Celebrity 3-tier), dailyRates.upcomingChange for date-effective rate changes (HAL 2026-06-01), top-level regions[] for region-priced lines (Costa, MSC), and childPolicy.ageMultipliers for tiered child rates (Costa under-4 free / 4-14 half / 15+ full). 13 of 15 lines remain unmodified after each extension. Future line quirks should follow the same shape: optional, additive, backward-compatible.

verification_meta.rationale: Protected operator anchor — household weight signal.
